quote: Matsumae no koto — Ezo kotoba no koto of c. 1600 with 117 words compiled for use by merchants
issue: The phrase 'Ezo kotoba no koto' appears to be a repetition or a mistranscription. It should likely be a single title or a clearer description.
suggestion: Matsumae no koto, a collection of Ezo words (c. 1600) with 117 words compiled for use by merchants

quote: ‹contested as to the interpretation of Ainu agency and objectives›
issue: The epistemic tag 'contested' is followed by a parenthetical explanation that is not a standard tag. This should be either a standard tag or integrated into the prose.
suggestion: ‹contested› (as to the interpretation of Ainu agency and objectives)

quote: the Kurile Ainu were relocated to Shikotan in 1884, and by 1933 only forty-one descendants survived — that variety of Ainu is now extinct
issue: The text states that the Kurile Ainu variety is now extinct, but then mentions that 'forty-one descendants survived' in 1933. This implies there were still people alive who were descendants of Kurile Ainu speakers, which contradicts the idea of the variety being extinct at that point.
suggestion: the Kurile Ainu were relocated to Shikotan in 1884, and by 1933 only forty-one descendants survived; that variety of Ainu is now extinct

issue: The place name 'Shikotan' is not consistently italicized or marked with a language tag, unlike other Ainu or Japanese terms in the text.
